title: Edo Period Street Life Visualizer
description: An interactive map recreating daily life in 1800s Edo (Tokyo), drawing from historical woodblock prints and merchant records.
story: In this project, I dove deep into ukiyo-e art and Tokugawa-era census data to reconstruct the bustling merchant districts of old Edo. I built a zoomable, clickable city map where users can explore life as a samurai, merchant, or craftsman.

title: Manga Sentiment Analyzer
description: A machine learning tool that analyzes emotional arcs across manga chapters using panel layout, typography, and translation data.
story: In this project, I trained a computer vision model on thousands of scanned manga panels to detect emotional tone — from the frantic speed lines of a shonen battle to the quiet stillness of a slice-of-life moment. The results were surprisingly nuanced.

title: Kanji Genealogy Tree
description: A visual web app tracing the etymological roots of over 2,000 kanji characters, from ancient Chinese oracle bones to modern Japanese usage.
story: In this project, I became obsessed with how a single brushstroke from 3,000 years ago could evolve into the characters on a Tokyo subway sign today. I built an interactive tree where each node is a living character with its full ancestral history.

title: Studio Ghibli Landscape Archive
description: A curated database mapping real Japanese locations to their fictional Ghibli counterparts, complete with cinematography notes and travel guides.
story: In this project, I spent months cross-referencing production artwork, director interviews, and geography to pinpoint the real hillsides, train stations, and coastlines that inspired iconic Ghibli scenes. Users can toggle between film stills and real photos.

title: Samurai Code Ethics Companion
description: An interactive study guide on Bushido philosophy, comparing historical texts like Hagakure with modern interpretations in anime and pop culture.
story: In this project, I explored how the code of the samurai has been romanticized, distorted, and reimagined across centuries — from Yamamoto Tsunetomo's original writings to Rurouni Kenshin. I built a side-by-side text comparison tool for students and enthusiasts.

title: Anime Opening Song Decade Atlas
description: A data visualization charting the evolution of anime opening themes from the 1970s to the 2020s, analyzing genre, tempo, and lyrical themes.
story: In this project, I scraped and analyzed audio features from over 1,500 anime OPs using Spotify's API and manual tagging. The result is a scrollable timeline that shows exactly when J-pop took over from orchestral scores, and when lo-fi crept in.

title: Meiji Restoration Digital Archive
description: A searchable, annotated archive of primary sources from Japan's modernization era (1868–1912), translated and contextualized for English-speaking researchers.
story: In this project, I worked with public domain documents — imperial edicts, newspaper clippings, and diplomatic cables — to build a research tool that lets historians filter by theme, date, and political faction. It filled a real gap in English-language resources.

title: Yokai Field Guide App
description: A mobile-first encyclopedia of Japanese folklore creatures, cross-referencing classical illustrations with their appearances in modern manga and games.
story: In this project, I catalogued over 300 yokai from Toriyama Sekien's 18th-century woodblock books and traced each one forward through Gegege no Kitaro, Pokémon, and beyond. The app lets you search by region, power type, or media appearance.

title: Japanese War Diary Translation Project
description: A collaborative humanities project digitizing and translating personal diaries from WWII-era Japan, preserving civilian and soldier perspectives.
story: In this project, I partnered with a small team of translators and historians to digitize fragile wartime diaries donated by Japanese families. We built a platform for crowdsourced translation review, ensuring accuracy while honoring the humanity behind each entry.

title: Shonen Power Scaling Database
description: A tongue-in-cheek but meticulously sourced database ranking power levels across major shonen franchises using in-universe feats and author statements.
story: In this project, I combined my love of Dragon Ball, Naruto, and One Piece with a surprisingly rigorous data modeling exercise. Every entry cites a specific chapter, episode, or databook. Debates in the comments section are, predictably, still ongoing.
